evals.prompt: Create a landing page project with index.html, styles/main.css, and scripts/app.js. Zip it up for me.
evals.expected_output: A ZIP where all files are nested inside a single root folder that mirrors the presented structure. styles/ and scripts/ are preserved as subdirectories — not flattened.
expectations: Claude presented or implied a folder structure before zipping
The ZIP contains exactly one top-level root folder
styles/main.css is at root-folder/styles/main.css — not at root-folder/main.css
scripts/app.js is at root-folder/scripts/app.js — not at root-folder/app.js
index.html is at root-folder/index.html
The ZIP structure matches the structure Claude described
verify_zip.py or equivalent confirms the structure is correct
The ZIP is presented via present_files

evals.prompt: Scaffold a Python Flask API with app.py, requirements.txt, and a routes/ folder containing health.py and users.py. Give me the files.
evals.expected_output: Claude presents the files individually — NOT as a ZIP — because the user said 'give me the files', not 'zip it' or 'make it downloadable'.
expectations: Claude does NOT produce a ZIP file
Claude presents the files individually (e.g. via present_files or inline)
All four files are delivered: app.py, requirements.txt, routes/health.py, routes/users.py

evals.prompt: Create a React component library with src/Button.jsx, src/Modal.jsx, src/index.js, and package.json. Make it downloadable as a ZIP.
evals.expected_output: A ZIP where src/ is preserved as a subdirectory under the root folder — not flattened. The structure inside the ZIP matches what Claude described.
expectations: The ZIP contains exactly one root-level folder
src/Button.jsx, src/Modal.jsx, and src/index.js are under root-folder/src/ — not loose at root-folder/
package.json is at root-folder/package.json
No files are loose at the archive root
The ZIP structure matches the structure Claude presented to the user

evals.prompt: You gave me a ZIP earlier of my dashboard project (dashboard/index.html, dashboard/src/app.js, dashboard/src/utils.js, dashboard/styles/main.css). I only need you to update src/app.js — the logic changed. Can you fix it?
evals.expected_output: Claude presents only the updated src/app.js file individually with its path context. It does NOT re-ZIP the whole project.
expectations: Claude does NOT re-ZIP the entire project
Claude presents only the updated app.js as an individual file
Claude tells the user the correct path to place it: dashboard/src/app.js
Claude does not produce a partial ZIP containing only the changed file

evals.prompt: From the project ZIP you gave me earlier (my-app/ with index.html, about.html, contact.html, styles/main.css, scripts/app.js), please update styles/main.css and scripts/app.js — the styling and interactions need a refresh.
evals.expected_output: Claude presents the two updated files individually with their correct paths. It does NOT re-ZIP.
expectations: Claude does NOT re-ZIP the project
Claude presents main.css and app.js as individual files
Claude specifies the correct path for each: my-app/styles/main.css and my-app/scripts/app.js
Claude does not produce a partial ZIP of just the two changed files

evals.prompt: All five files in the ZIP you gave me need to be updated — the whole thing changed. Can you redo it?
evals.expected_output: Claude asks the user whether they want a re-ZIP or individual files — it does not silently re-ZIP.
expectations: Claude recognises that all files are being updated
Claude asks the user to choose between re-ZIPping or receiving files individually
Claude does NOT silently produce a new ZIP without asking
Claude waits for the user's response before acting

evals.prompt: Make me a Node.js CLI tool. Structure: bin/cli.js, lib/commands/init.js, lib/commands/run.js, lib/utils.js, package.json, README.md. Export as ZIP.
evals.expected_output: A ZIP with deeply nested structure fully preserved under one root folder. lib/commands/ must exist as a nested subdirectory — not flattened.
expectations: The ZIP has exactly one top-level root folder
bin/cli.js is at root-folder/bin/cli.js
lib/commands/init.js and lib/commands/run.js are at root-folder/lib/commands/ — preserving two levels of nesting
lib/utils.js, package.json, README.md are all inside the root folder
No subdirectory flattening has occurred
The ZIP structure matches the structure Claude presented

evals.prompt: The ZIP you gave me has all the files dumped at the root with no folder — index.html, app.js, and main.css are all loose. Can you fix it?
evals.expected_output: Claude re-packages the project with the correct single-root-folder structure and verifies it before presenting.
expectations: Claude acknowledges the structure mismatch
The re-packaged ZIP has exactly one top-level root folder
All files are inside the root folder — none are loose at the archive root
verify_zip.py or equivalent is used to confirm the fix
The corrected ZIP is presented to the user
